了解核裂变和核聚变，了解核能的利用及核废料的处理。

deep_snippets.text: y is released. Once the size of the created nucleus exceeds that of iron, the short-ranging nuclear force does not have the ability to bind a nucleus more tightly, and the emission of energy ceases. In fact, for fusion to occur for elements of greater mass than iron, energy must be added to the system! Figure 22.29 shows an energy-mass curve commonly used to describe nuclear reactions. Notice the location of iron (Fe) on the graph. All low-mass nuclei to the left of iron release energy through fusion, while all highmass particles to the right of iron produce energy through fission. 22.66 22.67 Virtual Physics Nuclear Fission Click to view content (https://www.openstax.org/l/16fission) Start a chain reaction, or introduce nonradioactive isotopes to prevent one. ## Chapter Review Concept Items 23.1 The Four Fundamental Forces 1. What forces does the inverse square law describe? a. the electromagnetic and weak nuclear force b. the electromagnetic force and strong nuclear force c. the electromagnetic force and gravity d. the strong nuclear force and gravity 2. Do the carrier particles explain the loss of mass in nuclear decay? a. no b. yes 3. What happens to the rate of voltage oscillation within a synchrotron each time the particle completes a loop? a. The rate of voltage oscillation increases as the particle travels faster and faster on each loop. b. The rate of voltage oscillation decreases as the particle travels faster and faster on each loop. c. The rate of voltage oscillation remains the same each time the particle completes a loop. d. The rate of voltage oscillation first increases and then remains constant each time the particle completes a loop. 4. Which of the four forces is responsible for ionic bonding? a. electromagnetic force b. gravity c. strong force d. weak nuclear force 5. What type of particle accelerator uses oscillating electric fields to accelerate particles around a fixed radius track? a. ## Linac b. synchrotron c.

Relativistic Energy The original source of all the energy we use is the conversion of mass into energy. Most of this energy is generated by nuclear reactions in the sun and radiated to Earth in the form of electromagnetic radiation, where it is then transformed into all the forms with which we are familiar. The remaining energy from nuclear reactions is produced in nuclear power plants and in Earth’s interior. In each of these cases, the source of the energy is the conversion of a small amount of mass into a large amount of energy. These sources are shown in Figure 10.8. supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 核能与核反应

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: high
- **年级**: 12
- **章节/领域**: 近代物理
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「核能与核反应」的基本概念与方法
> 学业要求：能在真实情境中识别、运用「核能与核反应」解决问题
> 活动建议：通过观察、实验、练习、讨论等方式深化对「核能与核反应」的理解

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「核能与核反应」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「核能与核反应」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「核能与核反应」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「核能与核反应」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「核能与核反应」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「核能与核反应」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「核能与核反应」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以可观察实验或生活现象引入「核能与核反应」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
- 强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
- 围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「核能与核反应」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系工程、能源、交通或日常生活中的相关现象，讨论「核能与核反应」的应用边界。
- 补充科学史或技术应用案例，帮助学生理解物理规律如何服务真实问题。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
核能与核反应是物理学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「核能与核反应」的基本概念与方法”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以可观察实验或生活现象引入「核能与核反应」，先让学生描述现象，再提出可检验的问题。
2. 探究建构：强调控制变量、数据记录、图像分析和单位意识，避免只记公式不理解条件。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“高中12年级学段目标：理解并掌握「核能与核反应」的基本概念与方法”，设计预测—实验—解释—迁移的学习链条。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

a. The emitted red gluon is made up of a green and a red color. As a result, the down quark changes from a red color to a green color. b. Neutrinos are much more difficult for scientists to find when compared to other hadrons and leptons. Why is this? a. Neutrinos are hadrons, and they lack charge. b. Neutrinos are not hadrons, and they lack charge. c. Neutrinos are hadrons, and they have positive charge. d. Neutrinos are not hadrons, and they have a positive charge. 74. What happens to the masses of a particle and its antiparticle when the two annihilate at low energies? a. The masses of the particle and antiparticle are transformed into energy in the form of photons. b. The masses of the particle and antiparticle are converted into kinetic energy of the particle and antiparticle respectively. c. The mass of the antiparticle is converted into kinetic energy of the particle. d. The mass of the particle is converted into radiation energy of the antiparticle. 75.
